Description: Friendly label, e.g. AT-CRM-Integration / secret#2.
Stable hash used for upsert: {source}|{tenant}|{objectId}|{keyId}.
Which control plane the credential lives in. Option set: clm_sourcesystem.
What kind of secret. Option set: clm_credentialtype.
Entra application id, Key Vault object id, Power Pages site id, etc.
Sub-identifier when one object holds multiple credentials (e.g. passwordCredential.keyId).
Display name of the source object at discovery time.
Power Platform environment id, Azure subscription id, or tenant id.
Lookup to clm_sourceenvironment for richer scoping.
AAD tenant the source belongs to.
UTC datetime the credential expires. Searchable for fast filtering.
UTC datetime the credential becomes valid.
DiffInDays(Now(), clm_expirydate). Recomputed on read.
Lifecycle status. Option set: clm_credentialstatus.
Resolved owner systemuser. Populated by owner-resolver child flow.
Owning team for shared accountability.
When Yes, discovery flows do not overwrite owner fields.
Escalation target. Refreshed from Graph /manager weekly.
Updated by every discovery flow run that sees this credential.
Last time a Teams/email reminder was sent.
Buckets already notified. Option set: clm_reminderbucket.
Deep link to Planner task / DevOps work item created when owner clicks 'I'll renew'.
Deep link to manage the credential in its native portal.
0-100. Higher = more critical (production app, no fallback auth, etc.).
Free text notes from ops team.
If set, reminders are skipped until this date (used for decommissioned items pending cleanup).
